public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM-based 3-phase complementary PWM. Steps: 1) Enable the eGTM module clocks and verify the functional clock is present. 2) Configure three PWM channels (base + two synchronous) on the selected submodule for center-aligned operation at the required frequency. 3) Attach complementary outputs for each channel and set active high/low polarity pairing to generate complementary PWM signals. Assign the mapped output pins using the pin map configuration and configure them for push-pull output with an appropriate pad drive. 4) Configure hardware dead time for each complementary pair with the specified rising and falling delay values. 5) Configure a period interrupt for the base channel: set interrupt mode, provider (CPU0), and priority; assign the interrupt only to the first (base) channel while leaving other channels without interrupts. 6) Initialize the unified PWM driver with the main and per-channel configuration structures. 7) Enable synchronized shadow-to-active updates and start all configured channels synchronously so that frequency and duty are aligned across phases. 8) Initialize the LED GPIO as a push-pull output for visual indication and install the ISR handler that will toggle the LED on each PWM period event. Maintain basic status flags (e.g., pwm_initialized, dtm_configured, error_* flags) based on the success of clock checks, pin mapping, and channel initialization.

Apply runtime duty cycle updates for the three phases in a synchronized manner. Algorithm: 1) Read the requested duty array (three values for U, V, W) from the module’s shared state. 2) For each entry, clamp to the valid range [0.0 .. 1.0]; if any value is out of range, set an error flag and clamp it. 3) Set an update_in_progress flag, write all three duty values to the PWM driver using the synchronized multi-channel update API so changes take effect at the next PWM period boundary, then clear the update_in_progress flag. The function does not block for the period boundary; the hardware applies shadow-to-active transfer at the next cycle automatically.
